purpose: Define todas las consultas que el backend ISP necesita resolver contra las tablas tkc_*
principle: El backend nunca conoce la estructura interna del catálogo. Solo llama al Knowledge Reader con una consulta nombrada y recibe el resultado.

entities.name: Buscar entidad por nombre o alias
entities.use_case: El backend recibe un nombre desde la UI y necesita la entidad canónica
entities.sql: SELECT e.* FROM tkc_entities e LEFT JOIN tkc_aliases a ON a.entity_ref = e.id WHERE (e.canonical_name = :name OR a.alias = :name) AND e.vendor_id = :vendor AND e.catalog_version_id = :version

entities.name: Buscar entidad por id canónico
entities.use_case: Navegación directa cuando el id ya es conocido
entities.sql: SELECT * FROM tkc_entities WHERE id = :entity_id AND catalog_version_id = :version

entities.name: Listar entidades por tipo
entities.use_case: Poblar selectores en UI de provisión
entities.sql: SELECT * FROM tkc_entities WHERE entity_type = :type AND vendor_id = :vendor AND catalog_version_id = :version ORDER BY canonical_name

entities.name: Listar entidades por firmware
entities.use_case: Validar qué entidades existen antes de operar sobre un equipo con firmware específico
entities.sql: SELECT e.* FROM tkc_entities e JOIN tkc_entity_firmware ef ON ef.entity_id = e.id JOIN tkc_firmwares f ON f.id = ef.firmware_id WHERE f.version = :firmware AND e.vendor_id = :vendor AND e.catalog_version_id = :version

entities.name: Entidades con confidence menor a umbral
entities.sql: SELECT * FROM tkc_entities WHERE confidence_overall < :threshold AND vendor_id = :vendor AND catalog_version_id = :version ORDER BY confidence_overall ASC

commands.name: Todos los comandos de una entidad
commands.use_case: Saber qué comandos CLI puede ejecutar sobre una entidad
commands.sql: SELECT c.*, GROUP_CONCAT(cp.name) as params FROM tkc_commands c LEFT JOIN tkc_command_params cp ON cp.command_id = c.id WHERE c.entity_ref = :entity_id AND c.catalog_version_id = :version GROUP BY c.id

commands.name: Comandos por categoría
commands.use_case: Construir menú de acciones disponibles por entidad
commands.sql: SELECT * FROM tkc_commands WHERE entity_ref = :entity_id AND category = :category AND catalog_version_id = :version

commands.name: Comando con todos sus parámetros
commands.use_case: Construir y validar el comando CLI antes de enviarlo al equipo
commands.sql: SELECT c.*, JSON_ARRAYAGG(JSON_OBJECT('name', cp.name, 'type', cp.param_type, 'required', cp.required, 'example', cp.example)) as parameters FROM tkc_commands c JOIN tkc_command_params cp ON cp.command_id = c.id WHERE c.id = :command_id GROUP BY c.id

commands.name: Comando por firmware
commands.use_case: Evitar enviar comandos que no existen en el firmware del equipo conectado
commands.sql: SELECT c.* FROM tkc_commands c JOIN tkc_command_firmware cf ON cf.command_id = c.id JOIN tkc_firmwares f ON f.id = cf.firmware_id WHERE f.version = :firmware AND c.entity_ref = :entity_id AND c.lifecycle_status != 'removed' AND c.catalog_version_id = (SELECT id FROM tkc_catalog_versions WHERE vendor_id = :vendor AND is_latest = TRUE)
commands.variant_query: SELECT r.payload FROM tkc_results r WHERE r.result_type = 'variant' AND r.artifact_type = 'command' AND r.artifact_id = :command_id AND JSON_EXTRACT(r.payload, '$.firmware') = :firmware

commands.name: Comandos cuyo output mapea a un OID
commands.use_case: Dado un OID de alarma, encontrar el comando CLI para diagnóstico
commands.sql: SELECT c.*, cof.oid_ref FROM tkc_commands c JOIN tkc_command_output_fields cof ON cof.command_id = c.id WHERE cof.oid_ref = :oid_id AND c.catalog_version_id = :version

oids.name: Todos los OIDs de una entidad
oids.use_case: Construir lista de OIDs para monitoreo SNMP
oids.sql: SELECT * FROM tkc_oids WHERE entity_ref = :entity_id AND catalog_version_id = :version ORDER BY name

oids.name: OID por métrica
oids.use_case: Obtener OID string, scale y bit_calculation antes de SNMP GET
oids.sql: SELECT * FROM tkc_oids WHERE entity_ref = :entity_id AND id = :oid_id AND catalog_version_id = :version

oids.name: OIDs con bit_calculation activo
oids.use_case: El motor SNMP necesita saber qué OIDs requieren cálculo de índice
oids.sql: SELECT * FROM tkc_oids WHERE entity_ref = :entity_id AND bit_calculation = true AND catalog_version_id = :version

oids.name: OIDs por status
oids.use_case: Motor SNMP solo usa verified en producción, observed en diagnóstico
oids.sql: SELECT o.* FROM tkc_oids o JOIN tkc_catalog_versions cv ON cv.id = o.catalog_version_id WHERE o.status = :status AND cv.vendor_id = :vendor AND cv.is_latest = TRUE

oids.name: OID con escala y unidad
oids.use_case: Normalizar valor SNMP antes de almacenar o mostrar
oids.sql: SELECT oid_string, scale, unit, index_type, bit_calculation, index_def FROM tkc_oids WHERE id = :oid_id

relations.name: Prerequisitos de una entidad
relations.use_case: Obtener orden exacto de creación de dependencias antes de provisionar
relations.sql: SELECT e.*, r.creation_order FROM tkc_relations r JOIN tkc_entities e ON e.id = r.target_entity WHERE r.source_entity = :entity_id AND r.prerequisite = true AND r.catalog_version_id = :version ORDER BY r.creation_order ASC

relations.name: Todas las relaciones de una entidad
relations.use_case: Construir árbol de dependencias en la UI
relations.sql: SELECT r.*, e_target.canonical_name as target_name FROM tkc_relations r JOIN tkc_entities e_target ON e_target.id = r.target_entity WHERE r.source_entity = :entity_id AND r.catalog_version_id = :version

relations.name: Entidades que dependen de una entidad dada
relations.use_case: Antes de eliminar, saber qué se afecta
relations.sql: SELECT r.*, e_source.canonical_name as source_name FROM tkc_relations r JOIN tkc_entities e_source ON e_source.id = r.source_entity WHERE r.target_entity = :entity_id AND r.catalog_version_id = :version

relations.name: Equivalentes cross-vendor
relations.use_case: Encontrar equivalente Huawei de una entidad ZTE
relations.sql: SELECT a.cross_vendor_entity, a.cross_vendor_confidence FROM tkc_aliases a WHERE a.entity_ref = :entity_id AND a.status = 'cross_vendor'

alarms.name: Alarmas de una entidad
alarms.use_case: Obtener alarmas posibles cuando llega un trap SNMP
alarms.sql: SELECT * FROM tkc_alarms WHERE entity_ref = :entity_id AND catalog_version_id = :version ORDER BY severity DESC

alarms.name: Alarma por código
alarms.use_case: Traducir código de trap a información completa con remediación
alarms.sql: SELECT * FROM tkc_alarms WHERE code = :alarm_code AND entity_ref = :entity_id AND catalog_version_id = :version

alarms.name: Alarmas con threshold
alarms.use_case: Configurar motor de alarmas con umbrales del catálogo
alarms.sql: SELECT * FROM tkc_alarms WHERE entity_ref = :entity_id AND threshold_metric IS NOT NULL AND catalog_version_id = :version

alarms.name: Alarmas por severidad
alarms.use_case: Filtrar alarmas críticas para notificaciones inmediatas
alarms.sql: SELECT * FROM tkc_alarms WHERE entity_ref = :entity_id AND severity = :severity AND catalog_version_id = :version

alarms.name: OIDs relacionados a una alarma
alarms.use_case: Saber qué OIDs consultar para diagnóstico
alarms.sql: SELECT o.* FROM tkc_oids o JOIN tkc_alarm_oid_refs aor ON aor.oid_id = o.id WHERE aor.alarm_id = :alarm_id

metadata.name: Versión activa del catálogo
metadata.use_case: Knowledge Reader lo llama al inicializar
metadata.sql: SELECT * FROM tkc_catalog_versions WHERE vendor_id = :vendor AND family_id = :family AND is_latest = true

metadata.name: Cobertura del catálogo
metadata.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) as total_entities, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'verified' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as verified, AVG(confidence_overall) as avg_confidence, MIN(confidence_overall) as min_confidence FROM tkc_entities WHERE vendor_id = :vendor AND catalog_version_id = :version

metadata.name: Conflictos abiertos
metadata.sql: SELECT * FROM tkc_results WHERE result_type = 'conflict' AND resolved = FALSE AND vendor_id = :vendor AND catalog_version_id = :version

metadata.name: Huérfanos pendientes
metadata.use_case: Cola de revisión manual post delta processing
metadata.sql: SELECT artifact_type, artifact_id, payload, created_at FROM tkc_results WHERE result_type = 'orphan' AND resolved = FALSE AND catalog_version_id = (SELECT id FROM tkc_catalog_versions WHERE vendor_id = :vendor AND is_latest = TRUE) ORDER BY created_at DESC
